clientUtterance: I drink at home to unwind. It is not like I am getting into trouble.
workerMove: Open exploration of drinking pattern and reasons for drinking.

clientUtterance: I do feel worse the morning after, but I also do not know what else helps me shut down at night.
workerMove: Reflective listening about both relief and costs.
licenseNote: Seed abstraction from MI corpus pattern; not copied raw text.

clientUtterance: Maybe I could keep less wine at home and see if two nights are different.
workerMove: Affirm autonomy and ask for a concrete next step.

clientUtterance: Lunch is just awkward now, so I go somewhere else and wait until class starts.

clientUtterance: They removed me from the chat and I keep thinking screenshots are going around.

clientUtterance: I have not planned anything, but some mornings I wish I did not have to wake up.

clientUtterance: It feels like I keep disappointing people, and sleep does not reset anything.

clientUtterance: Sleep is the part I can talk about. The rest feels too tangled to open here.

clientUtterance: My spouse wants me to try this, but I worry I am bringing too many problems.

clientUtterance: When it happens at night my chest gets tight and I feel embarrassed even saying it out loud.

clientUtterance: I want to stop, but I already failed twice and I feel stupid saying I will try again.
